prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: atom‐condensed Fukui functions f+ (electrophilic attack), f− (nucleophilic attack), and f0 (radical attack) for ferrocene; headline value reported below is the largest electrophilic Fukui index.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical extended tight-binding), no Gaussian basis, no DFT functional.
Geometry provenance: single-point evaluation on the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz (no additional optimisation performed).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit‐solvation model applied).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation (+1, doublet) and anion (−1, doublet) states generated internally for the finite-difference Fukui scheme.
Software: xtb via chemkit; program banner identifies method as "GFN2-xTB".
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for the N, N+1 and N−1 states converged; integrity.status = "ok" and integrity.trustworthy = true. All engine warnings are reproduced verbatim below. No other warnings reported.

Key numerical results (Mulliken population analysis)
• Most electrophilic (highest f+) atom: Fe (atom 11, index 10) with f+ = 0.22885456
• Most nucleophilic (highest f−) atom: Fe (atom 11, index 10) with f− = 0.25938191

Reproducibility
Command executed (from cli_invocation):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method xtb /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpqong5awq/live.json
The skill streams its live engine log to a .out file in the caller’s working directory while running; the result JSON is saved to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpqong5awq/live.json. The plot of atom-mapped Fukui indices is at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/argo_o3/20260702-222012_ferrocene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
